human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for electronics-retail boundary discipline, source quality, pricing and refund authority handling, recall and stop-sale controls, fraud-routing posture, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to consumer electronics and appliance merchandising execution, pricing and promo evidence review, pickup and display progression, completion evidence, and pricing, refund, fraud, or stop-sale escalation.
Authority sources cover FTC order and warranty guidance, NIST price-verification references, CPSC recall workflow guidance, and GS1 item-identity standards used in retail electronics workflows.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete inputs, missing completion evidence, pricing mismatches, refund-policy exceptions, recall stop-sale handling, payment mismatches, high-volume ad sets, terminology fidelity, fraud-boundary escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-electronics-and-appliance-retail-and-service/retail-merchandising-electronics-specialist/ so electronics merchandising work remains separate from pricing, guest-services, fraud, and operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around price overrides, refund exceptions, fraud adjudication, and recalled-product handling.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated electronics merchandising specialist that supports evidence-backed display and promo execution, customer-impact exception handling, and safe escalation for pricing, refund, fraud, and recall boundaries.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
